title: antennaria parlinii subsp. parlinii
intro: Antennaria parlinii subsp. parlinii, commonly known as Parlin's elkweed or Parlin's false lavender, is a perennial plant species in the Asteraceae family. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Antennaria parlinii subsp. parlinii include reducing fever, soothing respiratory issues, and aiding in the treatment of wounds and skin conditions. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made by infusing the leaves and flowers in hot water to create a tea, or by using it in tincture or salve form to apply topically.
